sends email warning - still continues working - still charges --- # your direct answer if you do nothing: it will charge until: - your hard limit or - your card declines or - openai internal safety limits --- # my strong recommendation (do this now) go here: https://platform.openai.com/settings/organization/limits set: ``` soft limit: $10 hard limit: $20 ``` this makes your system safe. --- # what your codexclaw will do when limit reached telegram bot will reply something like: ``` request failed. check logs and try again. ``` and terminal will show: ``` insufficient_quota ``` system stops safely. --- # reality check: your trading agent cost estimate typical cost: research + strategist per day: $0.10 – $0.60/day monthly: $3 – $20 unless you spam it heavily. --- # bonus: how to see real-time usage go: https://platform.openai.com/usage you can see: - daily spend - token count - model usage --- # bottom line it will not secretly charge unlimited money if you set hard limit. your system will simply stop working when limit is reached. --- if you want, i can show you how to make your codexclaw automatically pause research when spending exceeds a threshold you define. || i get why you’re done — it’s still acting like a form/menu bot. the root cause is **not your router code anymore**. it’s that the strategist is **being invoked with extra “system-y” instructions somewhere (or an old strategist.md is still in effect)**, and it keeps defaulting to “give me inputs.” so let’s force the behavior in a way that **cannot drift**: ## the fix that makes it feel like chatgpt stop asking the strategist to be a router. make the **router deterministic in code**. ### new behavior: - your bot **always** chats as strategist (normal conversation). - it only calls research/execution/coder when *you explicitly say* “ask research…” / “monitor…” / “build…” - no route/final formats at all. - no menus, ever. this is how you get “free-form like chatgpt” today without fighting prompt weirdness. --- # copy/paste: replace your entire `bot.on("text", ...)` block with this in `src/bot.ts`, delete your current `bot.on("text"...` handler and paste this one: ```ts bot.on("text", async (ctx) => { const chatid = getchatid(ctx); if (!chatid) return; const usertext = ctx.message.text?.trim() ?? ""; if (!usertext || usertext.startswith("/")) return; await withtyping(ctx, async () => { const lower = usertext.tolowercase(); // simple, deterministic routing (no prompting needed) const wantsresearch = lower.includes("ask research") || lower.includes("ask researcher") || lower.includes("research this") || lower.startswith("research:"); const wantsmonitor = lower.includes("monitor") || lower.includes("manage my trades") || lower.includes("adjust stops") || lower.startswith("monitor:"); const wantsbuild = lower.includes("build") || lower.includes("dashboard") || lower.includes("add a feature") || lower.startswith("build:") || lower.startswith("coder:"); // if user explicitly requests a sub-agent, run it and then have strategist summarize. if (wantsresearch) { const research = await runpersona(chatid, "research", usertext); const strategist = await runpersona( chatid, "strategist", `user asked for research. summarize results conversationally and propose next steps.

research output:
${research.text}` ); await ctx.reply(stripfinalprefix(strategist.text)); return; } if (wantsmonitor) { const execution = await runpersona(chatid, "execution", usertext); const strategist = await runpersona( chatid, "strategist", `user asked for monitoring. summarize results conversationally and propose next steps.

execution output:
${execution.text}` ); await ctx.reply(stripfinalprefix(strategist.text)); return; } if (wantsbuild) { const coder = await runpersona(chatid, "coder", usertext); const strategist = await runpersona( chatid, "strategist", `user asked for development. summarize results conversationally and propose next steps.

coder output:
${coder.text}` ); await ctx.reply(stripfinalprefix(strategist.text)); return; } // default: just chat with strategist like chatgpt const strategist = await runpersona(chatid, "strategist", usertext); await ctx.reply(stripfinalprefix(strategist.text)); }); }); ``` this removes the entire “route” concept and guarantees free-form conversation. --- # also replace `personas/strategist.md` with this minimal version ```md # role: chief strategist chat naturally like chatgpt. no menus. no templates. no "reply with x". ask at most one clarifying question when needed; otherwise proceed with reasonable assumptions. if the user asks you to use research/execution/coder, you may summarize their output and help the user decide next steps. be concise, practical, and collaborative. ``` --- # then do 1) telegram: `/reset` 2) restart `npm run dev` --- ## why this will feel right because the bot will **always** respond conversationally. routing happens by simple keywords, not by a fragile prompt contract. --- if you still want “auto routing” later (true openclaw), we can reintroduce it after the system feels right — but this gets you working today with zero “pick option 1/2/3” nonsense. || yep — reset helped, but it’s still “router-y” because your **free-chat routerinstruction is forcing the strategist to talk in system-y formats**, and your strategist persona still has “routing format” emphasis. right now it’s acting like a switchboard, not like chatgpt. you want: **strategist chats normally**, and **routing happens invisibly in code**. so we’ll change one thing: ✅ **strategist should never show route/final to you.** ✅ strategist should output json only *internally* for the router, and your bot should never echo it. ✅ if no routing is needed, strategist just replies normally. ### the best fix: “hidden route” (in code), normal chat output to user --- # 1) replace your routerinstruction in `src/bot.ts` with this find `const routerinstruction = ...` and replace it entirely with: ```ts const routerinstruction = `you are the chief strategist chatting with the user.
` + `speak naturally and helpfully.

` + `if you need a specialist, output only this single line (and nothing else):
` + `route: {"calls":[{"agent":"research|execution|coder","task":"..."}]}

` + `otherwise, answer the user normally (no prefixes, no templates, no bullet menus).
` + `do not ask the user to choose options. ask at most one short clarifying question if truly necessary.
`; ``` this removes the “final:” requirement and stops it from offering menus. --- # 2) update the strategist persona to stop advertising routing menus open `personas/strategist.md` and make sure it does **not** include any “provide your request and i’ll route it” or list of lanes. use this minimal strategist persona (replace whole file): ```md # role: chief strategist you are the user's main assistant. chat naturally and help the user decide actions. you can delegate to specialist agents: - research: market/trade research, idea generation, style exploration - execution: manage existing trades only (hold / tighten stop / close). never opens new trades. - coder: dashboards/app maintenance. never proposes trades. behavior: - be conversational like chatgpt. - do not present menus or force templates. - if the user asks for research, delegate immediately with reasonable defaults and proceed. when you must delegate, output only: route: {"calls":[{"agent":"research|execution|coder","task":"..."}]} otherwise, respond normally to the user. ``` --- # 3) restart and reset once (so new behavior takes effect) in telegram: - `/reset` then restart your bot: ```powershell npm run dev ``` --- # 4) test with your exact request send: > “please research peter dicarlo’s trading style and summarize it for me.” expected behavior: 1) strategist outputs a hidden route 2) your code calls research 3) strategist synthesizes and replies normally no menus. no “pick one”. no templates. --- ## why your current output is still bad because your strategist is being instructed (by routerinstruction + persona) to behave like: - “i route requests. pick a lane.” we are replacing that with: - “i’m your strategist. i’ll just do it.” --- if you want, paste the current `personas/strategist.md` contents and i’ll point out the exact lines causing the menu behavior — but the replacements above will fix it immediately.

sampleMessages: the **http 503 service unavailable** error you're seeing on https://accounts.x.ai/data (or similar xai account pages) is a server-side issue. it means the xai backend is temporarily unable to handle the request—most commonly due to overload, a brief outage, maintenance, or scaling hiccups on their end. from what i can see right now: - xai's main status page (https://status.x.ai) shows no active incidents and lists services like grok (web), single sign-on, etc., as available. - that said, 503 errors can be intermittent or affect only certain sub-services/endpoints (like the data export/download part), even if the overall status looks green. ### what to try right now (user-side steps that often resolve or bypass temporary 503s): 1. **wait 10–30 minutes and retry** — these are usually short-lived. refresh the page or click "download" again after a break. 2. **try in a different browser or incognito/private mode** — sometimes cached data, extensions (ad blockers, privacy tools), or cookies cause weird conflicts with auth flows. 3. **clear browser cache/cookies specifically for accounts.x.ai and grok.x.ai** (or x.ai domain): - in chrome/edge: settings → privacy and security → clear browsing data → choose "cached images and files" + "cookies and other site data" → time range "all time" → clear (you can limit to just those sites if you want). - then log back in at grok.x.ai or x.ai and navigate to the data page. 4. **use a different device or network** (e.g., mobile data instead of wi-fi) to rule out any local routing/firewall weirdness. 5. **check if you're logged in properly** — sometimes session glitches cause partial page loads that trigger errors. log out completely, close the browser, reopen, and sign in fresh. if none of that works after an hour or two: - it's almost certainly on xai's side (not your account or connection). - you can check https://status.x.ai again for any updates. - as a last resort, reach out to xai support directly (usually via the grok interface → help/feedback, or look for a support link on x.ai). mention the exact url, the 503 error, your approximate time (around 12:29 am est), and that it's blocking data download. data exports like this are almost always processed asynchronously anyway, so even if the button works later, the actual file might get emailed or become available in the interface after some processing time. let me know if it starts working after one of those steps or if you get a different error message! || no, no raw/no-commentary video found. official ppa youtube upload (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hl9rwfhm-_8) has announcers. no silent version available. || yes, full match on youtube: "waters/johns v black/alshon at the zimmer biomet masters" (ppa tour channel).
